shape.pi_tui_render_strategy: differential render (3-strategy) + CSI 2026 synchronized output (atomic, flicker-free)

intake.interpretation: The hexa runtime today is a proof/effect-grading kernel, not a render kernel. pi-tui is a frozen 14-primitive surface with a formalizable diff-render contract. A design-seed for `hexa-lang TUI primitives` is the smallest credible artifact: declare the 14 candidate primitives, pin render-side invariants as falsifiers, and refuse to emit code this cycle. Honesty clause: no Tier-1 promotion claims for runtime-bridge (it does not yet exist) — only for design-shape items that survive their own falsifiers.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: hexa-runtime-tui-bridge-absent
falsifiers_preregistered.claim: The hexa runtime at /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/self/runtime.c emits no TUI/render primitives today; the only stdout-side contract is the `__HEXA_RC=<int>` sentinel for exit-code pass-through.
falsifiers_preregistered.verification: grep for tui|render|csi|ansi|cursor in /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/self/runtime.c returns zero TUI-render hits; only __HEXA_RC and effect-grading hooks present (Explore agent confirmed line 3274 hexa_exec_replace).
falsifiers_preregistered.implication: Any TUI-on-hexa story is a NEW BRIDGE, not an extension of an existing render kernel. Honesty clause: no Tier-1 promotion can claim runtime-bridge realization this cycle.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: pi-tui-primitive-set-frozen-and-citeable
falsifiers_preregistered.claim: /Users/ghost/core/hive/packages/tui exposes a stable set of 14 component primitives forked from @mariozechner/pi-tui (MIT, fork-cutoff 2026-04-25), suitable as the design-seed primitive table.
falsifiers_preregistered.verification: ls /Users/ghost/core/hive/packages/tui/src/components/ enumerates 14 .ts files; src/tui.ts is the engine entry; package metadata records hard-fork lineage with cutoff date.
falsifiers_preregistered.implication: Primitive-set discovery is import, not invention — design-seed enumerates the existing surface verbatim before proposing any reduction or extension.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: differential-render-contract-formalizable
falsifiers_preregistered.claim: pi-tui's differential render strategy (three-strategy diff + CSI 2026 synchronized output) can be expressed as a small set of falsifiable invariants over (frame_t, frame_{t+1}) pairs without referencing implementation details.
falsifiers_preregistered.verification: Invariants enumerated below: I1 only-changed-cells emitted; I2 atomic frame boundary via DECSET 2026 begin/end; I3 cursor-position restored at frame end; I4 no flicker observable when nothing changed (zero bytes emitted); I5 worst-case = full repaint (correctness-preserving fallback).
falsifiers_preregistered.implication: Render contract is portable — does not require hexa-runtime introspection to express. A future hexa-tui bridge can be checked against I1-I5 by externally-observable byte traces.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: keybindings-port-roi-witness-reusable-as-pattern
falsifiers_preregistered.claim: The 2026-04-26 keybindings-ts-port-roi witness (PASS, K=4 axes, 29 hexa-side test witnesses) provides a reusable design-strategy + falsifier-coverage shape for any future ts→hexa primitive port; not just keybindings.
falsifiers_preregistered.verification: /Users/ghost/core/nexus/design/kick/2026-04-26_keybindings-ts-port-roi_omega_cycle.json axes block + tier_promotions block lift cleanly when {keybindings} is generalized to {primitive}; companion artifact /Users/ghost/core/hive/core/keybindings.hexa (505 LOC) demonstrates 'bounded hexa mirror' is a real, testable shape.
falsifiers_preregistered.implication: Tier-1 reuse: `pattern_ts_to_hexa_primitive_port_witness` lifted from keybindings instance to general schema.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: design-seed-emits-no-impl
falsifiers_preregistered.claim: This cycle is design-seed only: zero .ts edits, zero new .hexa code, zero edits under /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/, zero edits under /Users/ghost/core/hive/packages/tui/. Only artifacts emitted are this witness JSON + state-side prompt/envelope log already on disk.
falsifiers_preregistered.verification: git status at cycle close shows only state/kick/runs/2026-04-27_hexa-lang-tui-primitive-design-seed/ + design/kick/2026-04-27_hexa-lang-tui-primitive-design-seed_omega_cycle.json as new paths; no edits to hexa-lang, hive/packages/tui, or hive/packages/coding-agent.
falsifiers_preregistered.implication: Honesty clause: design-seed = pre-implementation falsifier ledger. Implementation belongs to a follow-on `drill` stratum cycle, not this `smash` cycle.

render_contract_invariants.I1_only_changed_cells: for any frame transition (F_t → F_{t+1}), only grid cells with differing (glyph, fg, bg, attrs) are written to stdout
render_contract_invariants.I2_atomic_frame_via_csi_2026: frame body wrapped in ESC [ ? 2026 h … ESC [ ? 2026 l (DECSET begin/end synchronized output) so partial frames are never observable to a sync-aware terminal
render_contract_invariants.I3_cursor_restored: absolute cursor position at frame end equals the position requested by the caller (no leaked CUP from intra-frame movement)
render_contract_invariants.I4_zero_diff_zero_bytes: if F_t == F_{t+1} cell-for-cell, total bytes written for the transition (excluding the sync wrapper itself, which is also elidable) = 0
render_contract_invariants.I5_full_repaint_fallback: if diff strategy detects width/height change or escape-state corruption, fall back to full repaint; correctness preserved at the cost of bandwidth

primitive_table_seed.name: TruncatedText
primitive_table_seed.render: Text with explicit width clip + ellipsis

primitive_table_seed.name: Image
primitive_table_seed.render: Kitty/iTerm2 graphics protocol or unicode-block fallback

tier_1_gate.rule: Tier-1 promotion gated on (a) cited prior art exists AND (b) all referenced falsifiers PASS AND (c) no hidden implementation claim

candidates_explicitly_refused.reason: F1 PASS reveals no bridge exists today; promoting would conflate design-seed with implementation. Stays as `next_cycle` candidate, NOT Tier-1.
Reducing 14→N primitives requires usage data not collected this cycle. Stays as `future_drill` candidate.

design_strategy_findings.primitive_boundary: pi-tui's 14 components form a closed primitive set under the diff-render kernel; any new primitive must either compose existing ones (preferred) or extend the kernel (rare).
design_strategy_findings.kernel_surface_minimal: diff-render kernel needs only: write_cell(x,y,glyph,style), begin_frame(), end_frame(), get_size() — TUI primitives are user-space on top.
design_strategy_findings.hexa_bridge_shape_proposed_for_next_cycl: thin FFI shim: hexa side declares primitive ADTs + render(state)→cells; ts side runs the diff kernel and stdout. NOT IMPLEMENTED THIS CYCLE.
design_strategy_findings.honesty_clause: no claim is made that the proposed bridge will work; it is a design seed pending a drill-stratum implementation cycle with its own falsifier set.

falsifier_coverage_findings.render_axis_coverage: I1-I5 cover only-emit / atomic / cursor / zero-diff / fallback — sufficient for byte-trace external check
falsifier_coverage_findings.primitive_axis_coverage: F2 pins primitive count and lineage; reduction/extension would require new falsifiers in a follow-on cycle
falsifier_coverage_findings.ts_to_hexa_mirror_coverage: F4 lifts the keybindings-ts-port-roi pattern; coverage expressed as pattern-reusability, not single-instance correctness
falsifier_coverage_findings.scope_axis_coverage: F5 pins the design-seed/no-impl boundary, which is itself the load-bearing contract for this cycle's honesty
